character.chronicle: MINDS EYE THEATER ~ PILLARS OF SALT

rows: You had a mind brilliant for computer software engineering. Sadly, you finished your studies in a Soviet Union on the downhill slide. What should have been a high government post for you turned into a no-name job working for a company where you analyzed data and sent it on to higher ups for further processing. It was dull, mindless, and boring. So, perhaps more out of self preservation than anything, you discovered that in everything you were being asked to analyze, there were patterns. Sometimes they were blase ones, like the movement of money across global markets. But then, there were odd ones, strange ones. And one day, while studying the data, you found the existence of something you just couldn't believe was true: vampires. Away from work, you began your data collection, secretly of course, but the more information you gathered, the more you realized it was all true. Vampires walked among the human populace. You gathered and collated all the data you could, hoping to publish it one day. There was a problem, however, you were being watched. You knew it, knew your bosses at your mindless company knew it. Someone was watching you, and someone didn't want that information out there. So, you gathered what you could, wrote your article, and sent it to a university friend who worked in the press, asking him to publish it while you went into hiding. You knew they were tracking you down for this. No sooner did you get on the plane to get out of the country then they found you. Except the "they" weren't who you expected. Clan Tremere had to preserve the Masquerade, but more than that, they liked the talent you had. And they also knew the truth, that the company you worked for initially was a front for the New World Order, Technocratic mages who were using the reports you were filing to track the movements of other 'outside' elements, such as vampires. You've now taken the red pill, and you've been brought in. Seeing your value, the Clan sent you to their Moscow chantry for training, and while Technomancy isn't exactly the most attractive of all the paths of Thaumaturgy, your skill set alone has made you valuable in this high tech world. Now, rather than gathering the information on the monsters for the NWO, you are now gathering information on the NWO and others for the Tremere.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.
